is_p_falciparum.evidence: This is a human genetics study of the G6PD locus (human glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase gene) examining human nucleotide diversity. P. falciparum is only mentioned as the malaria parasite context, not studied as an organism.

reports_target_markers.evidence: The study reports human G6PD SNPs (e.g., A, A-, Med variants at amino acid residues 68, 126, 188). No P. falciparum drug-resistance markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, etc.) are reported.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Frequencies reported are for human G6PD alleles, not parasite drug-resistance codons.

sub_country_location.evidence: Human samples tied to sub-national ethnic groups/locations (e.g., Cameroon groups, Sierra Leone groups, Nigeria, Bakola Pygmies, Hadza, Maasai, Sandawe) — but these are human population samples, not parasite isolates.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No parasite sample collection dates given; the paper concerns archived human DNA samples.

african_field_samples.evidence: Samples are human DNA from African populations, not P. falciparum field isolates.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study sequencing G6PD in 216 individuals.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Not applicable to parasite drug-resistance database.

supplement.evidence: No parasite marker data exist in this human genetics paper.

assessment.summary: This is a human population genetics study of the G6PD gene examining nucleotide diversity and balancing selection related to malaria resistance. It does not study Plasmodium falciparum parasites or any P. falciparum drug-resistance markers; it concerns human host genetics.
exclusion_reasons: Not a P. falciparum study — analyzes the human G6PD gene, not the parasite
Reports no curated P. falciparum drug-resistance markers
Samples are human DNA, not P. falciparum field isolates
No parasite frequencies or collection dates
